pattern: Two independent linear formulas map a single Celsius reading to its Kelvin and Fahrenheit equivalents, returned together as a pair.
hints: Each target scale is just a fixed linear transform of the input value.
Kelvin shifts Celsius by a constant; Fahrenheit scales it then shifts it.
Return [celsius + 273.15, celsius * 1.8 + 32.0]; no rounding is needed since a tolerance is allowed.

edge_inputs.description: absolute lower bound of the input range
two-decimal value that yields a long fahrenheit fraction
upper bound of the input range

stress.description: many random readings across the allowed range
stress.note: single scalar input; tolerance judge absorbs floating representation

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def convertTemperature(self, celsius):
        kelvin = celsius + 273.15
        fahrenheit = 32.0
        step = celsius
        fahrenheit = fahrenheit + step + step * 0.8
        return [kelvin, fahrenheit]
